title_quote: Sphingolipid locus log2(CPM+1) heatmap (WT, Δfur Δsspb, intermediate, rescued); pathway is constitutive — neither induced nor suppressed — ruling out Fur-driven sphingolipid biosynthesis upregulation.
issue: Caption asserts the NB00 figure 'rul[es] out Fur-driven sphingolipid biosynthesis upregulation'. The figure is CPM-normalised and descriptive only — the speaker_notes for this very slide admit 'this orientation figure uses CPM normalization from NB00 and is descriptive rather than statistically DE-tested.' The actual statistical evidence (spt −0.64 FDR 0.002; sphk −0.40 FDR 0.02; 0/6 biosynthesis genes UP at FDR<0.05) lives in REPORT §Finding 4 and is presented on slides 16 and 18. A CPM heatmap cannot 'rule out' anything; only the FDR-tested NB04 panel can. The slide audience (who does not see speaker_notes) sees a strong rule-out claim that the visualised data do not license.

report_evidence.quote: Sphingolipid biosynthesis pathway constitutive (not induced) | 0/6 biosynthesis genes UP; *spt* DOWN -0.64 FDR 0.002; *sphk* DOWN -0.40 FDR 0.02 | SUPPORTED strongly
the sphingolipid biosynthesis pathway is *not induced* (rejecting the initial 'Δfur derepresses sphingolipid biosynthesis' framing)

fix_hint: Soften caption to 'pathway is flat across strains in NB00 orientation (CPM-descriptive); FDR-tested formal verdict on slide 16 (0/6 UP; spt −0.64 FDR 0.002)'. Or — preferred — replace this whole slide per F003 and move the orientation observation to a one-line bullet on slide 6.
